commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: feat(skills): add xero skill (official @xeroapi/xero-command-line)
target.body:
## What

Adds a bundled `skills/xero/` SKILL.md that exposes the **official Xero CLI** (`@xeroapi/xero-command-line`) to the agent. Mirrors the existing pattern used for `notion`, `github`, `gog`, and `1password` — npm/brew-installable third-party CLI, wrapped with OpenClaw `requires.bins` gating and a single `install` entry so the doctor flow can offer the install action.

The CLI is XeroAPI's official tool with browser-based PKCE OAuth, so auth stays on the user's side. The skill body focuses on what the agent must do *before* writes (`xero org details` first, profile/env-var checks, explicit user approval) plus the id-first workflow patterns that map cleanly to Xero GUIDs.

## Why

OpenClaw already bundles skills for the day-to-day SaaS the user is likely to be in: Gmail/Calendar/Drive (`gog`), Notion (`notion`), GitHub (`github`), 1Password (`1password`), Slack (`extensions/slack` via channel). Accounting is an obvious adjacent surface, especially for the "personal AI assistant" pitch — Xero is one of the two dominant SMB accounting platforms.

The Xero team publishes [`@xeroapi/xero-command-line`](https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-command-line) with a [SKILL.md](https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-command-line/blob/main/SKILL.md) explicitly designed for OpenClaw-style agents (it carries an `openclaw:` metadata block in its frontmatter). This PR adopts that skill into the OpenClaw bundle, condensed to fit the in-context budget and reshaped to match OpenClaw's bundled-skill metadata format. The full upstream is linked at the top and bottom of the file so the agent can pull more detail when needed.

## How

- `skills/xero/SKILL.md` — single file, no scripts or assets. Frontmatter is structurally identical to `skills/notion/SKILL.md` (compared byte-by-byte minus content).
- `requires.bins: ["xero"]` gates the skill to instances that have the CLI installed.
- `install` lists the official `@xeroapi/xero-command-line` npm package (`kind: node`, ships a `xero` bin).
- Safety preamble is preserved from upstream verbatim where it matters (write gates, identity verification, env-var warnings).

Nothing else in the repo is touched — no source code, no tests, no docs, no CHANGELOG. The intent is the smallest possible surface for a new bundled skill.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Adds a discoverable, install-gated bundled skill for the Xero accounting API. Without it, agents asked to do anything with Xero have to fall back to raw `curl` against the OAuth2 API, with no auth pattern or safety scaffolding.

**Real environment tested:** Local clean checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` at `701687efa3` (current `main`), macOS Darwin 25.5 (arm64), Node `v26.0.0`.

**Exact commands run after the patch:**

```bash
# 1. Structural validation against the in-tree skill validator.
python3.12 skills/skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py skills/xero/
# → Skill is valid!

# 2. Frontmatter parse check (matches sibling shape).
python3.11 -c "
import yaml, json
with open('skills/xero/SKILL.md') as f:
    fm = yaml.safe_load(f.read().split('---', 2)[1])
print(fm['name'])
print(json.dumps(fm['metadata'], indent=2))
"
# → 'xero'
# → metadata.openclaw.{emoji, requires.bins:["xero"], install:[{id:"node", kind:"node", package:"@xeroapi/xero-command-line", bins:["xero"], ...}]}

# 3. Diff vs the closest sibling (notion) — confirms only content differs.
diff <(head -23 skills/notion/SKILL.md) <(head -23 skills/xero/SKILL.md)
# → only the per-skill values differ (name/description/homepage/emoji/package). Shape is identical.

# 4. Verify the install target actually publishes the binary I claim.
npm view @xeroapi/xero-command-line name version bin homepage
# → name = '@xeroapi/xero-command-line', bin = { xero: 'bin/run.js' }, homepage = '.../xero-command-line#readme'

# 5. Hygiene.
tail -c 1 skills/xero/SKILL.md | xxd   # → 0a (final newline OK)
grep -n " $" skills/xero/SKILL.md      # → no trailing whitespace
```

**Observed result after fix:** The bundled validator marks the skill valid; the frontmatter parses to the same shape as the existing `notion` skill; the referenced npm package exists, publishes the `xero` binary I gate on, and points at the same homepage I cite.

**What was not tested:** No live OAuth round-trip against a real Xero org from inside an OpenClaw runtime — I don't have a Xero developer app set up for this account. The skill itself does not run code on its own; it tells the agent which `xero` subcommands to use. End-to-end coverage of the CLI behaviour lives upstream in `XeroAPI/xero-command-line`.

## Disclosure

AI-assisted (this PR was authored with Claude). Skill text is adapted from the upstream `XeroAPI/xero-command-line/SKILL.md` (MIT-style permissive context) with restructuring to match OpenClaw's bundled-skill format and condensed for context budget. Allow-edits-by-maintainers is enabled on the fork branch — maintainers are welcome to push fixes, rename, or trim sections directly.

## Checklist

- [x] No code changes outside `skills/xero/SKILL.md`
- [x] No CHANGELOG edit (maintainer responsibility per CONTRIBUTING)
- [x] Validates against `skills/skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py`
- [x] Frontmatter matches the structure used by `skills/notion`, `skills/github`, `skills/gog`, `skills/1password`
- [x] `requires.bins` and `install` reference a real published npm package (`@xeroapi/xero-command-line` v0.0.7)
- [x] Upstream source is credited prominently in the skill body
- [x] No symlinks; no auxiliary docs (README.md, INSTALL.md, etc.) inside the skill
- [x] American English in skill text
target.author: berkgungor
